Operation 'createPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks detail about input requirements and response structure. Added information about CreatePaymentRequest schema, PaymentResponse structure, payment ID generation, and state transitions to help AI agents understand what data to provide and what to expect in return.

data.current_description: Creates a new standard payment. Returns a gateway URL for customer redirection.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new standard payment transaction. Accepts payment details including amount, currency, payer information, and items via CreatePaymentRequest. Returns a PaymentResponse containing the payment ID and gateway URL for customer redirection to complete payment. The payment is created in pending state and transitions through status changes as the customer completes the payment flow.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks detail about input requirements and response structure. Added information about CreatePaymentRequest schema, PaymentResponse structure, payment ID generation, and state transitions to help AI agents understand what data to provide and what to expect in return.

message: Operation 'getPaymentStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description is too generic. Enhanced with information about the path parameter requirement, PaymentDetail response structure, and practical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Retrieves the current status and details of a payment.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current status and complete details of a payment by its ID. Returns a PaymentDetail object containing payment status, amount, payer information, transaction history, and other relevant payment metadata. Requires the payment ID as a path parameter. Use this operation to check payment progress, verify completion, or retrieve payment records.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic. Enhanced with information about the path parameter requirement, PaymentDetail response structure, and practical use cases to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'refundPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks critical details about input parameters and response structure. Added information about path parameter, request body requirements, OperationResult response, and state transitions to enable AI agents to properly construct and execute refund requests.

data.current_description: Refunds a payment. Supports full or partial refunds.
data.description_suggestion: Refunds a payment transaction, supporting both full and partial refund amounts. Requires the payment ID as a path parameter and refund amount details in the request body. Returns an OperationResult confirming the refund transaction. Full refunds reverse the entire payment amount, while partial refunds reduce the payment by the specified amount. Refunded payments transition to refunded or partially-refunded status.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical details about input parameters and response structure. Added information about path parameter, request body requirements, OperationResult response, and state transitions to enable AI agents to properly construct and execute refund requests.

message: Operation 'createRecurringPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description lacks context about the parent payment relationship and input/output details. Enhanced with information about RecurrenceRequest schema, RecurrenceResponse structure, and how recurring payments inherit from parent payments to help AI agents understand the recurrence setup process.

data.current_description: Creates a new recurring payment based on an existing parent payment.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new recurring payment transaction based on an existing parent payment. Requires the parent payment ID as a path parameter and recurrence configuration via RecurrenceRequest (frequency, interval, end date, etc.). Returns a RecurrenceResponse containing the recurrence ID and schedule details. The recurring payment inherits payment method and payer information from the parent payment and automatically processes subsequent charges according to the specified schedule.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks context about the parent payment relationship and input/output details. Enhanced with information about RecurrenceRequest schema, RecurrenceResponse structure, and how recurring payments inherit from parent payments to help AI agents understand the recurrence setup process.

message: Operation 'cancelRecurringPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal. Added details about path parameter requirement, OperationResult response, and important side effects (stopping future charges while preserving history) to help AI agents understand the cancellation behavior and consequences.

data.description_suggestion: Cancels an active recurring payment by its ID. Requires the recurring payment ID as a path parameter. Returns an OperationResult confirming the cancellation. Canceling a recurring payment stops all future scheduled charges while preserving the history of already-processed payments. The recurring payment transitions to cancelled status and no further transactions will be initiated.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added details about path parameter requirement, OperationResult response, and important side effects (stopping future charges while preserving history) to help AI agents understand the cancellation behavior and consequences.

message: Operation 'capturePreauthorizedPayment' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description lacks input/output details and context about preauthorization lifecycle. Enhanced with information about CaptureRequest schema, PaymentResponse structure, full vs. partial capture behavior, and authorization expiration context to help AI agents understand preauthorization capture workflows.

data.current_description: Captures a previously preauthorized payment. Supports full and partial capture.
data.description_suggestion: Captures a previously preauthorized payment, converting the authorization hold into a completed transaction. Requires the preauthorized payment ID as a path parameter and capture amount details via CaptureRequest. Returns a PaymentResponse confirming the captured payment. Full capture processes the entire preauthorized amount, while partial capture processes a lesser amount. Captured payments transition from preauthorized to completed status. Uncaptured authorizations may expire based on merchant account settings.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks input/output details and context about preauthorization lifecycle. Enhanced with information about CaptureRequest schema, PaymentResponse structure, full vs. partial capture behavior, and authorization expiration context to help AI agents understand preauthorization capture workflows.

message: Operation 'voidPreauthorization' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too minimal. Added clarity on what 'void' means in payment context, specified the required input (payment ID), explained the effect (releases funds, prevents capture), and included important constraint about capture status. This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Voids (cancels) a preauthorized payment by its payment ID. This operation releases the preauthorized funds and prevents any future capture attempts on the specified preauthorization. Requires the payment ID as a path parameter. Returns an OperationResult indicating success or failure of the void operation. Note: Voiding is only possible for preauthorizations that have not yet been captured.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added clarity on what 'void' means in payment context, specified the required input (payment ID), explained the effect (releases funds, prevents capture), and included important constraint about capture status. This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Generates and downloads an account statement for a specified period.
data.description_suggestion: Generates and retrieves an account statement for the authenticated merchant account covering a specified period. Accepts an AccountStatementRequest containing date range and filtering parameters. Returns the generated statement document (typically in PDF or CSV format depending on configuration). The statement includes transaction details, fees, and account activity for the specified period. Requires bearer token authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about inputs and outputs.
